question.text: Which of the following factors affect BOTH call and put option premiums in the SAME direction?

answer.text: Volatility and Time to expiry (both increase premiums when they increase)
Interest rates and Time to expiry

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Higher volatility → higher premiums for BOTH calls and puts (more chance of becoming ITM). Longer time → higher premiums for BOTH (more uncertainty). Spot price and interest rates affect calls and puts in OPPOSITE directions. **The Trap:** Spot ↑ → Call ↑ but Put ↓. Interest rate ↑ → Call ↑ but Put ↓. Only volatility and time affect both the same way. **Cross-Exam Context:** Factor sensitivity table is heavily tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember: Volatility and Time are always 'option-friendly' — they increase the value of both calls and puts.
